s.m. Burying, burial, interment; covering, hiding, concealing — dafn karnā, v.t. To bury; to hide, conceal
more: n. m. دفن hid; interred. Burial; interment. dafn k., dafnānā , v. a. To bury; inter. dafn h. , To lie buried.
s. m. Burial, hiding, concealing. دفن کرنا dafn karnā, To bury, to hide, to conceal. دفن هونا dafn honā, To be buried.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دفن (dafn), from Arabic دَفْن (dafn).

ety: dafn, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. aug.)+nā
def: v.t. To bury, &c. ( = dafn karnā).
wety: From دَفْن (dafn) + ـَانا (-ānā) by analogy with Classical Persian دَفْن کَرْدَن (dafn kardan). Compare Gujarati દફનાવવું (daphnāvvũ).

def: s. (dakh.) See دف daf.

ety: A. daftar, diftar, fr. the P.; Gr. διφθερα 'a skin,' &c.
def: s.m. A roll, scroll, list; an index; a bundle of papers or written documents tied together in a cloth; a record, register, journal, book, volume, account-book; an official statement or report (especially of public revenue); archives; a record-office; an office, counting-house, place of business
more: n. m. 1. Register; roll; archives; an official statement or report, especially of the public revenue. 2. A long story or report; a large volume. Ham to samjhe the likhegā tū koī harf, aë Mir! Par terā nāma to ek shauq kā daftar niklā . Mīr. 3. An office; office of records; place of business; counting house; agency. 4. An establishment. daftar-/?/kharch . Office charges. sar-i-daftar , The head of an office; a head clerk.
s. m. A book, volume, journal, record, register, account-book, archives, roll. دفترخانه daftar-ḵẖāna, s. m. An office, counting-house, archives. (Arab. Plur. دفاتر).
subs: s.m. Head of an office; head clerk.

def: s.m. An office-keeper; a man who has charge of the stationery, &c. of an office, and whose business it is to arrange and bind the papers and books, rule sheets of paper, make and mend pens, prepare envelopes, &c.
more: n. m. lit . an office keeper. One who has charge of the stationery of the office and arranges and binds the books, rules the paper, mends pens, and prepares envelopes, etc.

def: n. f. The boards of the binding of a book; paste-board.
s.f. The sides or boards (of the binding of a book); pasteboard.
more: s. f. The boards of the binding of a book, pasteboard.

def: s.f. The sides or boards (of the binding of a book); pasteboard.
more: s. f. The boards of the binding of a book, pasteboard.

def: v. a. دفع repelled. 1. To ward off; guard or provide against. 2. To avert; prevent; dispel. dafa-ul-vaqtī . Beguiling the time; a pastime. dafa h .; Wom. dafān h. , v. n. To be turned back, defeated, etc. Dafān ho! Wom. Take yourself off! dafā ho! dafān ho! intj. Be gone! be off!

ety: Zend dagha, fr. daz; S. दाह, rt. दह्, orig. दघ्; Prk. दाघ
def: s.m. A mark burnt in, a brand, cautery; mark, spot, speck; stain; stigma; blemish; iron-mould; freckle; pock; scar, cicatrix; wound, sore; grief, sorrow; misfortune, calamity; loss, injury, damage; — (adjectively) branded, cauterized, scarred, wounded, &c.
more: n. m. 1. A spot; speck; stain; mark; pock; cicatrix. Morī mailī gudaṛyā dho de, dhubiyā ke lāl! Auroṅ ke dhove sāl dusāle, dāg diloṅ dā dho de! Panj. song. Mārfat . My filthy rags O wash thou clean! Others their rich shawls have washed clean, My sinful heart O wash thou clean! 2. A blemish; fault. 3. A brand; stigma; bad name; obloquy. 4. A wound; sore. 5. Sorrow; grief. 1. Mujhe āj tak us bāt kā dāg hai . 2. Buṛhāpe meṅ beṭe kī maut kī dāg lagā . 6. Loss; damage. Chār rupaë kā dāg aur dīyā. dāg-bel , n. f. Marking off the lines for a road; a line of road; mark. dāg par dāg . Calamity on calamity. dāg-dār , adj. 1. Spotted; burnt; branded; scarred. 2. Stained; spoiled. dāg denā , v. a. 1. ( dāgnā ) To mark by burning with a hot iron; to cauterize; brand. Terā kāṭā na jīë koi dam kā, Dāg de gaī kale/?/ve meṅ hamkā . E. song. 2. To burn (a dead body). dāg k., kaṛkaṛānā , v. a. To heat ( ga…

1. s. m. Spot, stain, mark, scar, cicatrix, blemish, freckle, speck, a mark made by burning with a hot iron, brand, stigma, cautery. 2. (adjectively) Wounded, cauterized, having many scars. داغ بربالاي داغ dāg̠ẖ bar bālā-e-dāg̠ẖ, One misfortune following another. داغ چڙهانا يا لگانا dāg̠ẖ-chaṛhānā or -lagānā, To vilify, to defame. داغدار dāg̠ẖ-dār, adj. Scarred, cauterized, spotted, marked. داغ دينا dāg̠ẖ-denā, 1. To mark by burning with a hot iron, to cauterize, brand, scar. 2. To blemish. داغ لانا dāg̠ẖ lānā, To vilify. داغ لگنا dāg̠ẖ lagnā, To be damaged, to get a bad name. داغ هونا dāg̠ẖ honā, To be cauterized or scarred.
subs: dāg̠ bar bālāʼě dāg̠ = dāg̠-par dāg̠
s.f. Marking out the lines of a road with a spade; a line of road; mark
One misfortune following another, calamity on calamity
adj. Burnt; branded, cauterized; scarred; marked, spotted; streaked, striped; stained; blemished, spoiled
(-meṅ), To set fire (to), to set alight (as a pyre, &c.)
(-meṅ), To mark, to scar; to wound, to grieve
(-meṅ), To vilify, &c. = dāg̠ lagānā (-meṅ), To stain, sully, vilify, defame
(meṅ), To be burnt; to be branded; to be defamed, to get a bad name; to be damaged
v.n. To be cauterized, be scarred, &c.

def: s.m. = H داگنا दागना dāgnā, v.t. corr. of dāg̠; and dāg̠nā, qq.v.
more: s. m. For داغ dāg̠ẖ, q. v.

def: s.f. Pace, step, stride (also written ḍheṅg, deg)
more: s. f. A pace, one step (measure).
subs: ḍag dharnā, v.n. To step, step out, stride.

def: n. f. Cheating; trickery; imposture ( dagā ); mala fides ; villainy. Yārān chorī, na pīrān dagā-bāzī . Prov. Nor secret from friends, nor falsehood to your priest.

ety: prob. connected with dag̠l and dag̠dag̠a
def: s.f. Deceit, imposture, treachery; cheat, fraud, artifice, delusion
more: n. f. 1. Deception; imposition; treachery ( buttā ); cheat; artifice; delusion. Jinkī bolī meṅ 'dagā dagā' hai, unke dil meṅ kyā dagā na hogī? Prov. How full of guile their hearts must be when in their speech it is betrayed quite free! (Said of Kabulis in India who usually say ' ḍagā dagā ' (this, this) when they want to know the price of anything). Haë! bachche, ṭū ne abhī se dagā dī! Mah. Wom. Dirge over a dead child. Alas! my child! so soon betrayed by thee! dagā-bāz , adj. Deceitful ( chhaliyā ), dagā-bāz , n. m. 1. A cheat; swindler; rogue; sharper, Phaṭahā tilak, aur madhurī bānī, Dagā-bāz kī yehī nishānī . Prov. Sectarial stripes a lot, with accents soft and mild, The villain's tokens these by which you are beguiled. 2. A traitor; a knave. dagā denā yā k. , v. a. To impose upon; circumvent; defraud; act deceitfully or treacherously; play false; betray. dūsrā sha/?/khs ban-kar dagā…

s. f. Deceit, imposture, treachery, cheat, fraud. دغا باز dag̠ẖā-bāz, Deceitful, fraudulent, treacherous; cheat, knave, impostor, betrayer, traitor. دغا بازي dag̠ẖā-bāz-ī, s. f. Cheating, treachery, imposture, deceitfulness, knavery, foul play. دغا خوري dag̠ẖā-ḵẖor-ī, s. f. Treachery, deceit. دغا ديني dag̠ẖā denī, To deceive, cheat, or impose on. دغا کرني dag̠ẖā karnī, To act deceitfully or treacherously. دغا کهاني dag̠ẖā khānī, To be deceived, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, treacherous, fraudulent; — cheat, knave, impostor, traitor
s.f. Deceitfulness, fraud, imposture, cheating, knavery, treachery, foul play, villainy
s.f. The being deceived or imposed on; — deceit, treachery
(-se, or ke sāth), To act deceitfully or treacherously, to practise deceit or treachery, to play false, &c.
(-se), To be deceived or imposed on (by).

ety: dagadh˚ = S. दग्ध, p.p.p. of rt. दह्
def: v.t. To burn, scorch, singe; to hurt, wound; to tease, vex, torment, pester; to chide, upbraid, threaten; to revile.
more: v. a. 1. To burn; singe; brand. 2. To hurt; wound; prick.
v. a. 1. To burn. 2. To tease, to vex. 3. To chide, to threaten, to upbraid, to revile, to pester.

def: s.f. Pri e paid for branding or marking.
more: n. f. The price for marking or binding.

ety: v.n. fr. دغل; used in P. with amplification of signification
def: s.m. Corrupt state, corruptness, unsoundness; corruption, vice, depravity; deceit, fraud, treachery, villainy ( = dag̠ā); base coin; — a cheat, impostor, villain; a pickpocket; confusedness or entanglement of plants or herbage; abundance or luxuriance of herbage; luxuriant, tangled, or dense herbage; — adj. Unsound; base (as coin); corrupt, depraved; false, insincere, deceitful, treacherous
more: 1. s. m. Vice, corruption, treachery, depravity. 2. Luxuriant and entangled herbage. 3. An impostor, a villain, a pickpocket. 4. adj. False, deceitful. 5. Base (as coin). دغل فصل dag̠ẖal faṣal, Treachery, deceit, depravity.

def: adj. Deceitful, treacherous; — dag̠alī or dag̠lī, s.f. Deceit, fraud, treachery, villainy ( = dag̠al).
s.m. A combination of four short syllables.
v.t. To cause to be, or to have, branded or marked; — to cause (a gun) to be fired.
more: (from داغ or caus. of دغنا) v. a. 1. To cause to fire a gun. 2. To cause any thing to be marked.

def: s.m. A kettle-drum (cf. ḍaṅkā and ḍhakkā).

'To take another road or path'; to mislead, deceive, trick, cheat.

more: v. a. To disbelieve a true testimony.

more: adj. S. दग्ध burn. Shining; bright. n. m. A burning lamp placed within a paper cup.

ety: fr. A. دغدغة dag̠dag̠at, inf. n. f. of دغدغ 'to tickle,' &c.; used in P. with amplification of signification
def: s.m. 'Titillation'; inclination or prompting (of the mind); suspicion, doubt, distrust; mental disturbance, tumult, perturbation, perplexity, anxiety, solicitude, care; alarm, fear, dread, trepidation, awe.
more: n. m. Mental disturbance; perturbation; apprehension; alarm. Wasl kī rāt rahtā hai har dam, Dagdagā tā sahar judāī kā. dagal-fasal , n. f. Dissimulation; duplicity; double-dealing ( bhagal , 3).
s. m. Alarm, disturbance, tumult, fear, dread, awe.

= H ڐگڐگاکے डगडगाके ḍagḍagāke, = H ڐگدگانا डगडगाना ḍagḍagānā, See ḍakdakākar, and dakdakānā.
= H ڐگڐگاکر डगडगाकर ḍagḍagākar, = H ڐگدگانا डगडगाना ḍagḍagānā, See ḍakdakākar, and dakdakānā.

v. n. To glow, to gleam, to twinkle.

more: v. n. 1. To shake. 2. To burn brightly, to bu rn clear (charcoal). ڐگڐگاکرپاني پينا ḍagḍagākar pānī pīnā, To drink greed ily, or a large quantity at a draught.

def: = H ڐگڐگاکر डगडगाकर ḍagḍagākar, = H ڐگڐگاکے डगडगाके ḍagḍagāke, See ḍakdakākar, and dakdakānā.

v.n. To burn brightly, &c. ( = dhakdhakānā, q.v.). v.n. To blaze up, to burn brightly; — to shine, glitter, flash, twinkle; — to beat, throb, palpitate, flutter; to tremble, shake (through fear); to be perturbed, be anxious.
s.f. = dagdagāhaṭ, q.v. s.f. Brilliance, splendour; sparkling, sparkle, glow, twinkling, twinkle (as of a star).

subs: s.m. A black crow, a raven.

s.m. lit. 'Taking long strides,' or 'having long legs'; longshanks; a horse, &c. that takes long steps or strides, a lean long-legged horse, a Rosinante (also written ḍhaggā; cf. ḍheṅkā).
more: s. m. A lean, long-legged horse, a Rosinante, a garran.

def: s. f. Price paid for marking.
adj. Cheating, deceitful, treacherous.
adj. Marked, spotted, stained; branded, sullied, disgraced, dishonoured; spoiled, damaged
more: adj. 1. Spotted; stained; marked. 2. Spoiled. Dāgī ām . 3. Branded; disgraced; dishonored. dāgī k. , v. a. 1. To stain; spot. 2. To brand; pollute; disgrace; dishonor.
adj. Spotted, stained. داغي کرنا dāg̠ẖī karnā, 1. To stain, spot. 2. To blemish.
subs: v.t. To spot, to stain; to blemish; to brand, stigmatize, disgrace, dishonour.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Marked, spotted, stained ( = dāg̠-dār).

adj. Deceitful, &c. ( = dag̠al).
s.m. = دگلا daglā, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) A frock of quilted cloth, &c. ( Harris ): see دگلا daglā. ¶ dug̠ẖlā, Mongrel ( Harris ).

more: n. m. H. dhāgā thread, ghālna to put in. A quilted coat. Vāh vāh miyāṅ bāṅke! tere dagle meṅ sau sau ṭāṅke! Prov. Bravo! here comes a dashing blade! a hundred patches in his coat displayed!
s. m. A kind of quilted vest worn as armour.

def: s. (dakh.) A quilted mirzāʼī, for which see مرزاٴي mirzāʼī, sign. 2.

r: ḍagmag, ḍigmag, ḍigmig, ḍagmagā, ḍigmagā, ḍag-ḍag
def: adj. Shaking, shaky, tottering, trembling, tremulous, rolling, reeling, swaying from side to side; unsteady, wavering, vacillating.

adj. 1. Trembling; quivering; reeling. 2. Tottering; unsteady. Jis ghar būḍhā na baḍḍā, woh ghar ḍaggamḍaggā . Wom. Prov. The house in which no elders dwell a tale of ruin soon must tell. ḍagmag, yā ḍigmag chāl , n. f. A waving, flexive, wanton gait.

r: ḍagmagā, ḍigmagā, ḍag-ḍag, ḍagmag, ḍigmag, ḍigmig
def: adj. Shaking, shaky, tottering, trembling, tremulous, rolling, reeling, swaying from side to side; unsteady, wavering, vacillating.
s.f. Shaking, tottering, trembling, swaying, rolling; shivering, quaking.
n. f. Shaking; trembling; tottering.
v.n. = ḍagmagānā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. To totter; stagger; reel; tremble; shake. 2. To move with a twisting wanton gait, much affected by women. 1. Dekhī ek chatar nār, ḍagmag pag dharat jāt, dekhat sudh bhūl jāt, Kuch ko uchkāt jāt, adhran muskāt jāt, chhab dekh, pag ḍigmagāt . Kabit. A sprightly maid with wanton gait, my senses me forsook, Her bosom's swell, her lips all smiles, round bust—I trembling shook.
v. n. To tremble. See ڐگمگانا ḍagmagānā.

def: v.n. To shake, totter, tremble, stagger, reel; to shiver, quake, quail (with fear); to sway or roll (in walking), to move with a twisting wanton gait, to walk or move affectedly.
more: v. n. To totter, stagger, shiver.

ety: dāg̠, q.v.+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To mark by burning with a hot iron, to brand, to cauterize; — to fire (a gun, &c.)
more: v. a. 1. To fire (a gun, etc). 2. To brand.
v. a. 1. To cauterize, to mark by burning with a hot iron. 2. To fire (a gun, &c.).

def: v.t. = H داگ दाग dāg, s.m. corr. of dāg̠; and dāg̠nā, qq.v.
v.n. To be branded with a hot iron, to be marked; — to be fired off (as a gun).
more: v. n. P. dāg/?/ stain. 1. To be marked or branded. Ūṅṭ to da/?/gte the, makṛī (yā meṅḍakī) ne bhī ṭāṅg phailā dī! Prov. When the camels are branded, the spider (or frog) too must put up his leg! (Fable of the Frog and the Bull). 2. To be fired off (as a gun).
v. n. 1. To be fired off (as, a gun). 2. To be marked.

def: s.m. An impostor.
s.m. Road, way, highway ( = ḍagar).

n. m. Road; way; highway. Tū dagṛe meṅ kī beryāṅ, tere ber ku-ber! Tere tale ko ḍholā niklā, tane rākhā kyoṅ na gher! Song. Dohā . Oh way-side plum-bush! be from good fruit hence debarred! My love beneath you passed, why held you not him hard! dagṛā bagnā (Rus.), yā chalnā , v. n. To be used (a road).
more: n. m. Dim. ḍagrī , n. f. A bamboo tray for rice, flour, etc., used also for winnowing.

'To take another road or path'; to mislead, deceive, trick, cheat.

v.t. To rock; to roll; to propel ( = ḍagrānā).

def: v. a. To propel; to roll.
v.t. To put in motion, to propel, to roll.

def: s.f. dim. of dagrā, q.v.

ety: Prk. दाहो; S. दाहः, rt. दह्
def: s.f. Burning; combustion, conflagration; cauterizing; cautery; — fire; the sensation of burning, internal heat, feverish or morbid heat; inflammation; — ardour; heart-burning, envy, jealousy
more: n. f. S. दाह burn, Pr. ḍah . 1. Fire; flame. Nainā tohi paṭak daūṅ, khanḍ khanḍ ho jāë! Tan bich dāh lagāë-ke, āp alag ho jāë . Dohā. These eyes of mine I'll dash to pieces on the ground, Me set ablaze, aloof they heedless stand profound. 2. Combustion; conflagration. 3. The burning of dead bodies; cremation. Ehī bidhī dāh kriyā sab kīnhī: Bidhi-vat nahāë til-ānjalī dīahī . Rāmāyan. 4. A fire or burning within. 5. Ardour ( josh , 3.). 6. Envy; jealousy. Par sukh dekhī dāh ur antar; par dukh dekh harakh ghanero . Bhajan. Another's joys it burns his heart to see, another's griefs fill him with boundless glee. dāh denā , v. a. To light the funeral pile. dāh rakhnā , v. n. To be envious, jealous; to bear malice.
n. f. S. जग to burn. Heart-burning; jealousy; envy; malice.
s. f. Burning, conflagration, ardour. داه دينا dāh denā, To light the funeral pile. داه رکهنا dāh rakhnā, To grudge.
subs: (-ko), To apply fire (to), to light (the funeral pile)
v.n. To bear envy, &c., to be envious or jealous; to grudge
s.m. A place where dead bodies are burnt
s.f. The act, or the ceremony, of burning a corpse
s.m. 'Removing heat'; the root of the fragrant grass Andropogon muricatus (which is woven into screens and kept wet for the purpose of tempering the hot winds; see ḵẖas-ḵẖas).

ety: Prk. दहो; S. द्रहः, prob. a Prākrit form of S. ह्रदः
def: s.m. Very deep water, a deep pool; depth, abyss; pool or eddy (in a river), whirlpool, vortex; a lake; a river (e.g. kaṅwal-dah, 'A pool or lake of lotuses or water-lilies'; kālī-dah, 'the river Kālī').
more: n. f. Body. See بدن 1. 1. Brah anal tan tūl samīrā, Suāṅs jare chhain māhe sarīrā, Nain shraveṅ jal nij-hit lāgī Jare na pāv deh brah-āgī . Rāmāyan. Absence a fire, mere lath this frame, Each breath this frame this fire must claim, But tears of love all freely flow, Extinguishing the body's glow. 2. Nas ukhṛī sab deh kī, atī birhan, tan chhīn, Chaṭ āī paṭ paṛ gaī, pī ko ūpar līn . Riddle. Ans. Chaṭāī . A mat. Her veins plucked out, in absence grew her body lean, No sooner come, thrown on her back, her lover on her seen. 3. Apnī to yeh deh bhī nahīṅ . Prov. Not even our bodies are our own. deh dharnā , v. n. 1. To assume or come into a body. 2. To be incarnate. Deh dhare ke ḍanḍ haiṅ . Prov. A body made, the fine paid. (No body, no suffering). deh-ḍaṅḍ , n. m. Corporal punishment. deh'ī , adj. Corporal; physical ( jismī ).
n. f. 1. Very deep water. 2. A river ( naddī ). Kālī dah . 3. A whirlpool. 4. Depth; height.
n. m. A village. deh-bandī , n. f. A detailed statement of villages and their assessment.

adj. Ten ( das ). Dah dar dunyā, sad dar ākhirat . Mah. Prov. Charity will be returned sevenfold in the next world. dah-chand , adj. Ten times; tenfold. dah dar dah , Ten cubic yards (of water), held pure in Mah. law ( hadīs ). dah-sāla , adj. Decennial. dah-nīmī , n. f. lit . half of ten. Five per cent. dah-yak , n. m. The tenth part; one in ten; a tithe. dallū kā dahserā , More com. ḍallū kā dasahrā , The ten-seer weight of Dallū, applied to any incongruity. One who obtrudes observations foreign to the matter in hand. Dallu was a grain merchant who used a weight of ten seers, whereas the ordinary weight is the paserī or five-seer weight.
s. m. Very deep water, abyss. کنول ده kaṅwal-dah, A place abounding in water lilies.

ety: Pehl. dah; Zend daśan, nom. daśa; S. दशन्, दश
def: adj. Ten (also used, for daś or das, by some of the old Hindī poets, as Ćand, &c., and in Hindī compounds)
subs: (bāshī, fr. T. bāsh, 'head'), s.m. A commander of ten men; a person having a place of trust; a valet
s.f. A ten-fold (return, — used in returning a salutation)
dah dar dunyā ṣad dar āḵẖirat

prov. 'Ten in this world, a hundred in the next'; charity will be returned ten-fold in the next world (used as a benediction by mendicants; — sattar, 'seventy,' is sometimes substituted for ṣad, 'hundred')
'Ten by ten,' ten cubic yards (of water, — such being regarded as pure according to the ḥadīs̤ or Mohammadan law)
adj. 'Having ten hearts'; fickle, inconstant, faithless; — brave, courageous, bold
s.m. An aggregate of ten days, a decade
adj. Of or for ten years; — s.f. A book comprising the collections, accounts, registers, &c. of ten years
s.m. A weight of ten ser or seers
adj. & s.m. Consisting of, or capable of carrying, ten men; — a company or body of ten men; a cart (smaller than a gāṛī or ćhakṛā), capable of carrying ten men
adj. Holding ten man or maunds
s.f. lit. 'Half of ten'; five per cent.
s.m., or dah-yakī, s.f. A tenth part, a tithe, one in ten; an allowance of ten per cent.

ety: Prk. डाहो or दाहो; S. दाहः, rt. दह्; — or fr. ḍāhnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Burning, rage, heart-burning, jealousy, envy, malice, spite, rancour
more: (from ڐاهنا ḍāhnā, q. v.) s. f. Malice, spite, envy, jealousy, burning.

def: s.m. The first ten days of the Moharram (syn. ʻashara; ʻāshūrā); — the models of the tombs of Hasan and Husain, or the model of a building containing the tombs, which are carried about in procession during those ten days. (On the tenth day the tombs are buried, or in some places, thrown into water: the building, if made of cheap materials, shares the same fate; if costly, it is carried back and deposited in the dargāh or Karbalā.)
more: (from ده Ten) s. m. 1. The first ten days of the Muḥarram: called also عشره and عاشورا 2. The models of the tombs of Ḥasan and Ḥusain, or the model of a building containing the tombs, which are carried about in procession during those ten days. On the tenth day the tombs are buried, or in some places thrown into water: the building, if made of cheap materials, shares the same fate: if costly, it is carried back and deposited in the dargāh or Karbalā.

def: s.m. corr. of dahā, q.v.
more: (from ده Ten) s. m. The ten days of the muḥarram, in which the tombs of Hasan and Husain are carried about and thrown into the river or other water. See دها dahā.

def: s. (dakh.) Heat: see گرمي garm-ī.
s.f. The figure ten; tenth part; the tens, the tens' place (in numeration), the decimal place (of figures), a decimal.
more: adj. S. अर्द्धद्वय. Two and a half. Mere pās āë to uskī chhātī par chaṛh-kar, ḍhāī chullū lahū pī jāūṅ! Wom. If she ever comes within my reach I'll sit on her breast and drink two and a half handfuls of her blood! ḍhāī ghaṛī kī ānā ; Wom. v. n. lit . to die in 2½ hours. To die very soon. Rām jī kare! tujhe ḍhāī ghaṛī kī ā jāë! Hin. Wom. Rām grant you may die in two hours and a half! ḍhāī-ghare , n. m. One of the highest of Khatrī gots or races.

def: n. f. S. दशन् Tens; a decimal.
(from ده) s. f. 1. The figure ten. 2. The tenth part. 3. The decimal places of figures in arithmetick, the tens.
s.m. corr. of dihez, q.v. s.m. Dowry, portion; a wife's paraphernalia, &c. (see jahez).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Burning, kindling, setting on fire; causing heat or combustion; incendiary; inflammatory; cauterizing, caustic; — an incendiary; — the plant Plumbago zeylanica.
s.f. Burning, blazing, conflagration; glow; ardour, fervency, fervour.
s.m. Cave, cavern; abyss; pitfall.
more: s. m. A cavern, abyss, a pitfall.

def: n. m. A cavern; abyss; pitfall. (?).
s.m. The place of tens (in Numeration).
more: n. f. Butea frondosa . The bark yields an astringent exudation, called. Palās-goṅd , the wood is used for coating wells, and for the sacrificial fire. The flowers are bright red and yield a yellow or orange dye. Jahāṅ ḍhāk, wahāṅ ḍākū . Prov. In Dhak woods dark robbers lurk. ḍhāk-pachchhi, ḍhak-pachhe . The man who collects the gum from ḍhāk trees. ḍhāk'ā , n. m. A thick wood of ḍhāk trees.
(from ده Ten) s. m. The place of tens in arithmetick.

def: s.m. A collection or aggregate of ten; — the tens' place, the tens (in Numeration).
n. m. 1. Blow; shock; loss; damage. 2. Grief; sorrow.
more: n. m. S. दशक A group of ten; tens.

def: s.m. Loss, damage; blow, shock; grief, sorrow, affliction.

ety: dahak˚ = Prk. दहक्क(इ), or दहक्के(इ), fr. S. दह+कृ
def: v.n. To burn, to blaze; to be burnt; to be ruined or destroyed, to be lost; to regret, grieve, be consumed with regret, &c.
more: v. n. S. स्थग 1. To be covered. 2. To be concealed.

v. a. To cover; conceal. 1. Āg ko dāman se ḍhāṅknā . Prov. To cover a fire with one's skirt. (To cherish a serpent). 2. Oṛhnī oṛhe ek paṭī, Gaurā chhātī ḍhake, nā pet . Wom. Song. She wears a shẹet but one breadth wide her breasts and stomach both exposed!
n. m. Dim. ḍhaknī , n. f. A lid; cover.
v. n. 1. To bend forward ( jhuknā , 1.); peep; prick up one's ears; eavesdrop. 2. To take aim; point at. 3. To enter; penetrate; thrust in; force one's way. 4. To return; come back.
v. n. 1. To burn, to be burnt, to be ruined, lost, destroyed. 2. To regret.

def: v.t. = ḍahkānā, q.v. v.t. To burn, kindle; to heat (iron, &c.), to inflame; to cause to regret or grieve; to ruin, destroy, lose.
v. n. Pr. dadḍham . To blaze up (See jalnā , 1, 3, 5).
n. f. Fear; dread ( bhaë , 1).

s.f. Shaking tremulously, shaking, trembling, quaking, tremor; fear, apprehension, dread; — shifting sand, quicksand, &c. ( = daldal). (The name of Dahlī or Dehlī is said to be derived from this word; the ground on which the city was built being so loose and infirm that tent-pins could not be fixed in it.)

ety: dahal˚, prob. = Prk. दहड = S. दह+ल or र
def: v.n. To shake, tremble, quake; to fear, be alarmed or terrified
more: v. n. 1. To incline; lean; bend; turn towards. Kyoṅ sāhab! hamārī hī taraf ḍhulte ho ! 2. To be carried, conveyed, transported. Asbāb ḍhulnā . 3. To go forward. 4. To give; let go; let slip.

v. n. 1. To run down; flow. 1. Muṅḍe sir par pānī paṛā ḍhal gayā . Prov. Water runs off a bald head. 2. Kānā to kājal kare, ganjā mal mal nhāë, Kachhve kī sī khoprī pānī ḍhal ḍhal jāë . Bald heads are scrubbed, the one-eyed use lampblack, The water runs off from a turtle's head or back. 2. See ḍhālnā , v. a. 3. To decline; fade. 1. Haif, aīyām javānī ke chale jāte haiṅ! Har ghaṛī din kī tarah ham to ḍhale jāte haiṅ . Inshā. Alas! youth's spring time just flits past away, I dwindle as declines each hour of day. Nazīr. 2. Jab āṅkh se sūraj kī ḍhalā rāt kā kajrā . When from the sun's eye fell its mantle of lampblack. din ḍhalnā , v. n. To decline (the day). ḍhaltī phirtī chhāoṅ, lit . the changing shadow; The changes of fortune.
v. n. To shake, to tremble, to fear.

def: v. n. To tremble with fear.
s.m. A collection or aggregate of ton (e.g. tīn dahām tīs).
adj. Burnt to ashes, burnt out, extinguished.
adj. Turned to ashes; extinguished.
s.m. Causing to burn, setting fire to, burning, inflaming; cauterizing.
s.m. The mouth; an orifice.
more: s. m. The mouth, an orifice.

def: s.m. (but f. in Hindī), Fire; burning, combustion, conflagration; sultriness; — s.m. Agni, the god of fire; lead-wort, Plumbago zeylanica; the marking nut, Anacardium officinarum; — a gold coin (in value six rupees)
subs: (˚na+up˚), s.m. 'Fire-stone'; the sun-gem; a crystal lens

def: s.m. contrac. of dahān, q.v.
v. a.1. To cause to flow, to set afloat. 2. To burn (esp. the dead). Murde ko dahā do .
more: v. a. To cause to be milked.

def: n. m. P. دﮩـان mouth. 1. The mouth. 2. The mouth of a water-bag, river, drain, or water-course. 3. A curb or bit. 4. Pudendum fœminœ. Huīṅ samdhan jo mujhe dekh-ke p/?/nī pānī, Pānī kyoṅkar na churāëgā dahānā unkā . Siṭhnā. Marriage song. dahāna kholnā , v. a. 1. To open a water-course. 2. Mah. Wom. To take away one's virginity.
s.m. Mouth (of a water-bag, or drain, or watercourse, or river, &c.), opening (of a valley, &c.); — bit (of a bridle); anything which covers the mouth.
more: (from دهان dahān ) s. m. 1. The bit of a bridle. 2. The mouth of a water bag, river, or water-course. 3. Any thing which covers the mouth.

def: v.t. To set fire to, to burn
more: v. a. S. ध्वंस 1. To break; batter; shatter; knock down. 2. To bring upon one. Āfat ḍhānā. ḍhā denā , v. a. To pull down; demolish; raze. chunī chunāī imārat ḍhānā , v. a. To pull down one's fairy palace; overthrow one's project; spoil one's little game.
(causal of دهنا from दहन ) v. a. To burn.

def: s.m. See s.v. dahan. s.m. Causing to burn, setting fire to, burning, inflaming; cauterizing.
n. f. S. हाद to roar. A shriek; a loud cry.
s.m-1˚ = ḍagar, q.v.; — 2˚ = ḍahrā, q.v. s.m. A kettle-drum (cf. ḍaṅkā and ḍhakkā).
s.f. Roaring, thundering; roar, loud noise or cry
subs: dahāṛeṅ mār-kar ronā, To cry aloud, to weep bitterly ( = dāṛh mār-kar ronā).

def: n. m. S. ह्द 1. See ḍābar , (1, 2.). 2. A ship or boat's bottom. 3. See ḍagar , (2.) 4. A pool; a tank.
s.m.? = dahrī, and ḍahrī, q.v. s.f. Stiff clay soil (in low ground), marshy soil.
s.m. = dehir, q.v. s.m. Land flooded after the rains, marshy land.
v. n. To roar (as a tiger); to thunder. Are muë! kyoṅ dahāṛtā hai? Wom. dahāṛeṅ mārnā, dahāṛeṅ mār-kar ronā , v. n. To cry aloud or bitterly.
v. n. To roar (as, a tiger).
s.m.f. Astonishment, amazement, consternation; — adj. Astonished, amazed, stupefied, confounded, bewildered, struck with consternation.
more: 1. s. Astonishment, amazement. 2. adj. Stupified with amazement, struck with astonishment or consternation.

def: adj. Burning, scorching.
(Pers. ده Ten, and Turk. باش Head) s. m. A commander of ten men, a person having a place of trust, a valet.
adj. (f. -ī), Glowing, bright, blooming, flourishing, fresh, verdant, green, pleasant, pleasing; overpowering, excessive, great.
more: adj. Flourishing, blooming, green, fresh; pleasing.

def: adj. 1. Flourishing; blooming; green; fresh; vivid; glowing. Lage malne us gul-badan kā badan, Huā ḍahḍahā āb se woh chaman . Mir Hasan. 2. Bright; gay; shining. Jab kāṭo tab ḍahḍahā, aur sīṅchat hī kumlāë, Vārūṅ aise phūl ko jo phūl bel ko khāë! Riddle. Khusro. Ans. Chirāg a lamp. You cut it and it blooms; you water it, it dries, Should I a flower bless whose tendril 'neath it dies? 3. Large; great; excessive.
v. a. To flourish; blossom; glow.
v.n. To glow, shine; to bloom, blossom, flourish.
more: v. a. To flourish, to blossom.

def: s.f. (m.?) = daldal, q.v. s.f. Marshy land, mire, mud, marsh, swamp, bog, quagmire, slough, quicksand.
s.m. pl. = dahā, q.v.
more: E. n. m. P. dah ten. 1. The ten days of the Moharram , in which effigies of the tombs of Hasan and Husain are carried about and finally thrown into the water, but usually buried. 2. The effigies or tāziās themselves. Āj dahe nikleṅge .

def: s.m. corr. of dihez, q.v. s.m. Dowry, portion; a wife's paraphernalia, &c. (see jahez).
s.m. = daheṛ, q.v. s.m. A raven; a crow; a species of bird, Coracias; — the dial bird of Southern India, Copsychus saularis (a beautiful songster common throughout India).
more: n. f. S. शिथिल loose, Pr. sidhilo . 1. The state of being loose; slackness; laxity. 2. Remissness; delay ( ālkas , 2). ḍhīl denā , v. n. To loosen; relax; let go; give. ḍhīl k. , v. a. To dilly dally; dawdle.
s. m. The name of a bird. (Co. racias).

def: s.m. (dialec.), The ten (at cards).
more: adj. 1. Loose; flabby. 2. Unrestricted; unfettered; unshackled. 3. Slack; lax; dull; lazy. 4. Slovenly; clumsy. 5. Weak in the loins; impotent. 6. Slow; dilatory; remiss. ḍhīlā pāëṅchā , n. m. Loose trousers. ḍhīlā paṛ jānā , v. n. To become loose, weak, lax. ḍhīlā-pan , n. f. 1. Looseness; slackness; dilatoriness. 2. Clumsiness. ḍhīlī ḍorī ḍālnā , v. n. To let the reins hang loose; to grant one liberty or licence; to let alone. laṅgoṭe kā ḍhīlā , n. m. izār-band kī ḍhīlī , n. f. A rake; a woman of easy virtue.

n. m. 1. ( ḍhem, ḍhīm ) A clod of earth. 1. Man bhāve to ḍhelā bhī supārī . Prov. If you like it a clod is a betelnut. 2. Bāt kyā kahtā hai, ḍhelā sā mārtā hai! Not words but blows he deals! 2. Eyeball. Dard ke māre ḍhele nikle paṛte haiṅ. ḍhelā lenā , v. n. See note under استنجا istanje kā ḍhelā , n. m. A worthless person.

def: s.f. = dihlī or děhlī, q.v. s.f. Threshold ( = dihuṛī).
s.f. A vessel in which milk is coagulated, or in which coagulated milk is kept.
more: (compound of دهي from दधि , and हंडी A vessel) s. f. A vessel in which sour milk is kept.

def: s.m. A vessel for carrying coagulated or sour milk (especially at festivals).
more: s. m. A vessel for carrying sour milk in (particularly, at festivals).

def: s.f. A vessel in which milk is coagulated, or in which coagulated milk is kept.

ety: prob. fr. S. दग्ध; but cf. ḍheṛ
def: s.m. A raven; a crow; a species of bird, Coracias; — the dial bird of Southern India, Copsychus saularis (a beautiful songster common throughout India).
more: s. Name of a bird. (Perhaps, for دهيل dahel, q. v.). ¶ dhīṛ (dakh.) See ڐهيڙه ḍhīṛh.

def: s.f. = daheṅḍī, q.v. s.f. A vessel in which milk is coagulated, or in which coagulated milk is kept.
n. m. Dowry (جـﮩيز). dahez'ū ; Hin. dahejū , adj. Belonging to a dowry; hence, gim-crack, brummagem. Dahezū chīz kam ṭhairtī hai . What's given in a dowry does not last.
adj. Of or belonging to a dowry; — flimsy, gim-crack.
adj. (f. -inī), Burning, setting on fire; burning hot; tormenting, paining.
more: (part. act. of دهي) adj. Shrewd, ingenious, penetrating.

ety: act. part. n. of دهی 'to possess intelligence'
def: adj. Possessing intelligence or sagacity, cunning, knowing, skilful, shrewd, penetrating.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Jealous, envious, malicious, spiteful, rancorous; — a malicious person, &c.
n. m. S. दधि curdled milk; Pr. dahim ; Pālī, dahi . Coagulated milk; tyre. Le dahī! Street cry. Buy my tyre! daheṅ'ḍī , n. f. Cont. of dahī kī hāṅḍī , n. f. A vessel for holding dahī .
adj. Envious; malicious, etc.
s.m. Thick sour milk, coagulated milk (called 'tire or tyre' in India)
subs: v.n. To be or become coagulated (milk)
(f. -ī), A seller of dahī or coagulated milk.

def: adj. Right (hand, &c.) = dahinā, q.v.
more: adj. Right (opposite to left. Sans. दक्षिण South or right; because the earth, the navel of which corresponds to Hindūstān, has its head to the west, its feet to the east, the right side to the south, and the left to the north; or, perhaps, from the position of eastern people in adoration of the sun. In Arab. شمال denotes the north or left: and, this agrees with the manner in which the Roman augurs determined the regions of the heavens, according to Livy, I. 18.).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀸𑀳𑀺𑀡 (dāhiṇa), from a derivative of Sanskrit दक्षिण (dákṣiṇa), from Proto-Indo-European *deḱs-. Cognates include Early Assamese ডাহিন (ḍahino), Ancient Greek δεξιός (dexiós), and Latin dexter.

def: adj. (f.-ī), Right (hand, or side, &c.)
subs: dahnc obl. of dahnā; Prk. dāhiṇe; S. dakshiṇe)
adv. To the right; on the right; at the right.

def: part. Burnt, scorched.
n. m. A reservoir for irrigating high lands.
n. m. P. ده village, گان lord. A husbandman.
Wom. adj. Much; plentiful.
v.t. To lead astray, to mislead; to deceive, disappoint, baulk, tantalize, &c. ( = bahkānā, q.v.).
more: v. a. 1. To deceive, disappoint, balk, tantalize. 2. To spoil, throw away uselessly. ¶ ḍhukānā, v. a. caus. of ڐهکنا ḍhuknā, q. v.

def: v.t. To throw away uselessly, to spoil (cf. ḍahnā; ḍhānā).
v. a. Caus. of دﮩـکنا q. v.
v.t. To burn, kindle; to heat (iron, &c.), to inflame; to cause to regret or grieve; to ruin, destroy, lose.
more: (caus. of دهکنا) v. a. 1. To burn, to kindle. 2. To ruin, destroy, lose. 3. To cause to regret. 4. To heat (iron, &c.).

def: adj. Boorish; clownish ( dehātī ).
n. f. Rusticity; country manners.

n. m. H. das ten. Ten (of cards).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jagas in the Upper Doāb.
s.m. corr. of dalān, q.v. s.m. A hall

v.t. To shake, to agitate; to cause to tremble or quake.

def: n. f. A threshold. dahlīz jhāṅknā, yā lāṅgnā , v. n. To cross the threshold; pay one a visit. dahlīz kā kuttā , 1. A house dog; a bow-wow. 2. A hanger on; a parasite. dahlīz na jhāṅknā, lit . not to see across one's threshold. To be in strict pardah or privacy. dehlī ke leo le ḍālnā , v. a. lit . to wear out the plaster of the threshold. To come very frequently to one's house (a creditor, etc.).

ety: = S. देहली; — A. dihlīz, fr. the Pers.

more: (prop. dihlīz ) s. f. A portico, a threshold. (Adopted in Arab. Plur. دهاليز).
subs: s.m. The dog of the porch, house-dog; — a hanger-on, parasite
s.m. lit. 'Treading the threshold'; a ceremonial visit paid by a young man about to be married to the parents of his intended bride
(-kī), To cross the threshold (of), to pay a visit (to).

def: v.n. To be burnt; to be inflamed; to burn, blaze.
more: adj. Right, the opposite of left.

def: v.n. = ḍhanā or ḍhahnā, q.v. v.n. To run, go quickly, make haste, to rush; to bustle about, to toil, labour, drudge.

ety: ḍāh˚ = Prk. डाह(इ) or डाहे(इ), fr. डाह = S. दाह
def: v.n. To be hot, to burn; to burn with rage or envy, &c., to be malicious, or spiteful; to be heated or fused (as metals).
more: v. n. 1. To be malicious, to burn with spite. 2. To be fused (as, metal). 3. To heat (metal).

def: v. a. To burn; set fire to; light.
more: (from jom to eat), adj. S. दक्षिण The right (hand or side). It is also called the south because the earth, the navel of which corresponds to Hindustān, has its head to the west, its feet to the east, the right side to the south, and the left to the north; or perhaps, from the position of eastern people in adoration of the sun. Dāhnā dhove bāëṅ ko aur bāyāṅ dhove dāhne ko . Prov. The right hand washes the left, the left the right. (We all have to depend on one another).
v. n. 1. To pass through the fire (metal). 2. To burn with envy, etc.

def: v. n. Pālī, daḍḍho . 1. To burn; inflame. Yeh dukh-dāh dahe nit chhātī, Bhūkh na bāsar, nīṅd na rātī . Rāmāyan. This flame keeps ever burning in my breast, No care for food by day, at night no rest. 2. To sink; fall. 1. Zamīn dah gaī . 2. Kūṅā dah gayā . The well has fallen in.
v.n. (Braj.) = dahnā, q.v. v.t. To set fire to, to burn, to kindle.
adj. & s.m. To be burnt, fit to be burnt; combustible; — anything to be burnt.
n. m. Ten handfuls of grain.
s.m. A measure of about ten handfuls (of grain, &c.).
s.m. Tenth part, tithe, ten per cent., an allowance or tax of ten per cent.
more: (from ده Ten) s. m. Tythe, tenth part.

def: n. m. S. दशोत्तर Ten per cent.

s.m. Time; a long period of time; an age; eternity; fortune, fate; chance, adverse fortune, misfortune, calamity, adversity; danger; — custom, habit, mode, manner; care, solicitude; the world; atheism.
more: n. m. Cross buttock (Wrestling). Ḍhuṛ par uṭhā-kar de marā. ḍhuṛ par uṛānā , v. a. To throw on one's back. ḍhuṛ par chaṛhānā , v. a. To lift one's opponent on to the hip.
s. m. 1. Time, an age, custom, habit, mode, manner, care, solicitude. 2. The world. 3. Atheism. 4. Fortune, chance, adversity, danger.

def: s.m. A hole dug for water; hollow or low ground (in which water collects), tank, &c. ( = ḍabrā, ḍābar, q.v.); a water-tank (in a ship), a barrel or cask in which drinking water is kept (in a boat or ship); the part of a boat or ship in which bilge-water collects.
n. f. Stiff clay soil (in low ground).
more: n. f. A marshy soil. Sab se ghanī ubaj gaī mhārī, Rī sakhyāṅ! dhartī ḍahrī . Wom. Rus. song. My crop's come up the best, the marshy soil's bequest.

def: adj. Relating to time, or to fortune; secular, temporal; relating to the world, worldly; impious, atheistical.
s.f. Stiff clay soil (in low ground), marshy soil.
more: adj. Relating to time or fortune.

def: adj. Marshy; — s.f. Marshy soil, &c. ( = ḍabrā).
s.m. = دهريه dahrīya, q.v.
n. m. A. dahar time. A materialist; free-thinker; an atheist.

ety: for A. دهرية, fr. dahrī, q.v.
def: s.m. One who maintains the eternity of matter, a materialist, an atheist.

adj. & s.f. See s.v. dah, 'ten.' s.f. Burning; combustion, conflagration; cauterizing; cautery; — fire; the sensation of burning, internal heat, feverish or morbid heat; inflammation; — ardour; heart-burning, envy, jealousy

def: adj. & s.m. Giving, bestowing; giver, &c. (com. used at the end of comp., e.g. dukh-dāʼī, q.v.).
more: n. f. Cor. of P. دايه S. अपत्यादा from अपत child, दा to give. 1. (W.) A midwife; a monthly nurse; a sick-nurse. 1. Dāī ke sir phūl pān . Prov. The burden of presenting pān and green herbs rests on the midwife. (Green herbs prognosticate prosperity, and any misfortune which may happen to the newly-delivered woman or her new-born child is set down to the midwife's remissness in making the customary presentation). 2. Khur khāṅsī, terī dāī ke gale meṅ phāṅsī! Nursery rhyme. Begone you nasty cough! hang your midwife! (Native midwives put their fingers down the throat of the infant to enlarge the windpipe, the cough being here ascribed to the midwife as having failed in this part of her duty). 3. Dāī jāne apnī hāī . Prov. The midwife thinks the lying-in-woman is, like her (free from pain). 2. The stand that children run to in playing at hide and seek. Dāī rī dāī! tere sātoṅ bhāī! O stand! subs: s.m. An age of the demons (consisting of 12,000 divine years or the sum of the four Yugas or ages of man)
s.m.f. The place or source of demons.

ety: fr. ديث 'to be soft, or easy'
def: s.m. A wittol, a tame cuckold; one who winks at the adultery of his wife or the fornication of his female relations from interested motives.
more: n. m. A contented cuckold; one who winks at the adultery of his wife or female relations from interested motives.
s. m. A contented cuckold, or one who winks at the fornication of his wife or female relations from interested motives.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَیُّوث (dayyūs), from Arabic دَيُّوث (dayyūṯ).

def: s.m. = daiyūs̤, q.v. s.m. A wittol, a tame cuckold; one who winks at the adultery of his wife or the fornication of his female relations from interested motives.
s.m. A dark night; darkness, obscurity.
more: (from دجا) s. m. 1. Darkness, obscurity. 2. A dark night.

def: s.m. A liar, a great deceiver; — the false Christ, Antichrist; — adj. & s.m. Having one eye; — a one-eyed man.
more: n. m. 1. A one-eyed man; the antichrist. 2. A liar; an impostor.
(from دجل Chald. דבל He lied, put on a false appearance) 1. A liar, an impostor. 2. Antichrist.

def: s.f. = dākh, q.v. s.f. Grape; a raisin
s.m. Woollen cloth from which hairs hang, a coarse kind of woollen cloth (such as that of which a faqīr's blanket is made); — a fine and precious sort of cloth.
more: n. f. دق become thin. A hectic fever ( tap-i-diq ); chronic fever; merasmus. diq , adj. Vexed; troubled; worried; tormented. diq-dārī , Illit. n. f. 1. Trouble; worry; plague; vexation; annoyance; distress. 2. Narrow circumstances; poverty; penury. diq-dārī uṭhā'nā , Illit. v. n. To take trouble. diq-kar mārnā, diq k. , v. a. To tease; trouble; annoy; pester; worry; harass; torment. diq h. , v. n. To be irritated, etc.
adj. Subtile, minute, thin. تپ دق tap-i-daḳḳ, s. f. A hectick fever.
s. 1. Woollen cloth from which hairs hang. 2. A precious sort of cloth.

ety: prob. fr. S. द्राक् 'quickly,' or v.n. of ḍāknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Post (for conveyance of letters), the mail; disposition or relay of horses, or runners, or pālkī-bearers along a road (for conveyance of the post or travellers); travelling by post; a post-office
more: s. f. 1. A post (for the conveyance of letters, also relay of horses or of palkī bearers). 2. A postoffice. 3. Perpetual vomiting. ڐاک لگني ḍāk lagnī, To be seized with constant vomiting.
s. m. Husband of a ڐاکني ḍākinī, q. v.
subs: s.f. A post station, a station or stage at which a relay (of horses, or runners, &c.) is posted (for conveyance of the mail, or travellers)
s.m. Expenses incurred in travelling by post; postal charges; postage
s.m. A horse used for a ḍāk or relay; (fig.) a worn-out horse; — a fast walker
s.f. Mail-cart; mail-train; a carriage used in travelling by ḍāk
(-kī), To post relays (of horses, or runners, or pālkī-bearers)
v.n. A post to be organized; a ḍāk or relay (of horses, &c.) to be laid
adv. In the manner of the post, postingly, post-haste, without stopping on the way.

ety: prob. S. दाहकः; rt. दह्; cf. ḍahnā
def: s.m. A piece of gold or silver leaf, &c. placed under a gem (in a ring) to heighten its brilliance, a foil; a coloured substance placed under glass to form a mock gem ( = ḍāṅk).
s.m. The husband of a ḍākinī, q.v.; a wizard, sorcerer.
s.m. = ḍaṅk, q.v. s.f. The post or mail, &c. ( = ḍāk, q.v.). s.m. Giver, donor, one who makes presents (esp. to Brāhmans); the institutor of a sacrifice who employs and pays the officiating priests.
s.m. Giver, donor, one who makes presents (esp. to Brāhmans); the institutor of a sacrifice who employs and pays the officiating priests.
more: n. f. 1. The post; mail. Hai hichkiyoṅ kī ḍāk barābar lagī huī . Zauq. 2. Relays of men or cattle for the post. 3. Constant vomiting. ḍāk bolnā , v. n. To bid at an auction. ḍāk-bahṅgī , n. f. Parcel post. ḍāk-chaukī , n. f. A stage or station where a relay is posted. ḍāk/?/-khānā, ḍāk ghar , n. m. A post office. ḍāk kā kharch, ḍāk kā mahsūl , n. m. Postal charges; postage. ḍāk kā ghoṛā , 1. A horse used on dâks or relays. 2. A used-up horse. 3. One who walks fast. ḍāk-gāṛī , n. f. The mail train. ḍāk lagā'nā, yā biṭhānā , v. a. To post relays of cattle or runners. ḍāk lagnā , v. n. 1. To be laid (a ḍāk . or relays of men or horses); to be organized (a post). 2. To be seized with constant vomiting. 3. To feel excessively thirsty. ḍāk meṅ chiṭṭhī lagānā, yā ḍālnā , v. a. To post a letter. ḍāk-nīlām , n. m. An auction sale.

def: s.f. Continual vomiting; — calling out, shouting, &c.; bidding (at an auction)

ety: Prk. डक्कओ or डक्कअं; S. दष्ट+कः or कं
def: s.m. Robbery, gang-robbery; an attack by a band of robbers; a band or gang of robbers
more: s. m. An attack by robbers. ڐاکا پڙنا يا دينا يا ڐالنا ḍākā-paṛnā, or -denā, or ḍālnā, To rob.
subs: v.n. An attack to be made by robbers or 'dacoits,' 'dacoits' to fall (upon)
ḍākā ḍālnā, To commit 'dacoity' or robbery in gangs
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀟𑀸𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*ḍākka) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), of uncertain origin; see there for more.

def: n. m. An attack by a gang of robbers or bandits. ḍākā paṛnā , v. n. To be attacked by Dacoits. ḍākā paṛne-vālā , G G. Dacoity about to be committed. ḍākā ḍālnā ; Rus ḍākā ghālnā , v. a. To commit dacoity. Kit se is ne khānne lāūn, kit ghālūṅ /?/ā-kur ḍākā; Maryo yoh khasam akaṛ bāṅkā! Rus. Wom. Food where am I to get, or whom am I to spoil ? Death take a husband good for side and show, not toil! ḍākā-zanī , n. f. Dacoity; high way robbery.
s. plur. (of دقيقه) 1. Subtilties. 2. Difficulties. 3. Flour, meal. 4. Minutes, moments. 5. Minutiæ.
s.m. pl. (of daqīqa), Subtleties, nice points; difficulties; minutiæ; minutes, moments; — flour, meal (in this sense used in P. for the A. sing. daqīq).
s.m. = ḍakait, q.v.

ety: ḍākā, q.v.+ait = āʼit = Prk. आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्, the term. of the pres. part. with the caus. aug. āpi
def: s.m. One of a gang of robbers, a 'dacoit,' robber, brigand, highwayman, pirate ( = ḍākū).
more: s. m. A robber, a pirate.

def: n. m. See ḍākū .
s.f. = ḍakaitī, q.v.

ety: the preceding+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Gang-robbery, 'dacoity,' highway-robbery, robbery, piracy.
n. f. Gang-robbery; dacoity.
s.f. pl. (of dukkān), Shops.
n. f. S. उद्गार belching. 1. Bellowing; roaring; callingaloud. 2. A belch; eructation. ḍakār na lenā , v. n. lit . not to belch. Not to make the least sign; not to betray oneself.
s.f. A belch, eructation; — roaring, roar (of a tiger, &c.); bellowing, lowing (of an ox)
more: s. f. A belch, eructation, bellowing.
subs: 'Not to belch,' to make no noise, give or make no sign, to keep one's own counsel, not to betray oneself.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀟𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀭 (*ḍakkāra), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀟𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀭 (*ḍakkāra). Cognate with Punjabi ਡਕਾਰ (ḍakār) / ڈکار (ḍakār), Nepali डकार (ḍakār), Marathi ढेकर (ḍhekar), डकार (ḍakār), Kashmiri ڈاکُر (ḍākur).

ety: Prk. डक्कडी and क्कडडओ; S. दष्ट+र, and र +कः
def: A strong, rich, clayey soil (regarded as the best, or second best, quality of soil; but requiring much ram to saturate it sufficiently for sowing).

n. f. A strong, rich, clayey soil, breaking up into large clods, containing minute micaceous particles, and requiring abundant rain to saturate it sufficiently for sowing. Ḍākar dhartī, phāli khūṅḍī, dhorī kāṅdhī ḍāle sai, Kimaiṅ bāh nāh kare kamerā, yūṅeṅ din ne ṭāle sai . Rus. The ground is hard, the ploughshare blunt, the cattle quite bent down, No ploughing will the ploughman do, away his labour thrown.

ety: ḍakār˚ = S. उद्गार, rt. गृ with उद्
def: v.n. 'To belch'; to roar, bellow, low, make a loud noise; — v.t. To take down with a noise, to gulp down, to swallow; to embezzle
more: v. n. 1. To belch. 2. To bellow, to low. ڐکار بيٿهنا يا جانا ḍakār-baiṭhnā or -jānā, v. n. To embezzle.
subs: ḍakār-jānā (with acc.), To swallow up; to embezzle, make away with, misappropriate.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀟𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀭𑀇 (*ḍakkāraï, “to belch”), from Prakrit *𑀟𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀸𑀭 (*ḍakkāra, “burp, belch”). Related to ڈَکار (ḍakār, “burp”). Urdu verb set ڈَکَرْنا (ḍakarnā) ڈَکْرانا (ḍakrānā) ڈَکارْنا (ḍakārnā) ڈاکارْنا (ḍākārnā)

def: v. n. 1. To roar; bellow. 2. To belch. 3. To swallow. 4. To embezzle; misappropriate.
E. n. m. S. डकपुत्र = डक the progenitor, पुत्र son. A caste of Hindūs descended from a Brahman father and a guālin mother. They are skilled in astrology ( paṛyā , 2.).

s.m. A caste of Hindūs descended from a Brāhmin on the father's and a gwālin on the mother's side (they subsist on alms asked on Saturdays, and are skilled in astrology and fortune-telling, &c.); an individual of this caste.
more: s. m. A cast of Hindūs descended from a brāhmaṇ on the fatherʼs and a gwālin on the motherʼs side. They subsist on alms on Saturday, and are skilled in astrology.

def: s.m. A caste of Hindūs descended from a Brāhmin on the father's and a gwālin on the mother's side (they subsist on alms asked on Saturdays, and are skilled in astrology and fortune-telling, &c.); an individual of this caste.
more: s. m. A cast of Hindūs descended from a brāhmaṇ on the fatherʼs and a gwālin on the motherʼs side. They subsist on alms on Saturday, and are skilled in astrology.

def: adv. With loud or large gulps, in great draughts

def: adv. With loud or large gulps, in great draughts
subs: v.t. To drink with loud gulps, or in large draughts.

def: v.n. = ḍagḍagānā, q.v. v.n. To burn brightly, to shine, glow, gleam, twinkle; — to be inflamed, be very red.
v. a. To drink great draughts. Ḍakḍakā-ke pānī pīnā burā hotā hai .

ety: by redupl. of ḍak; see ḍakār; and cf. ḍhoknā
def: v.n. To make a noise in swallowing, to take large or loud gulps; to drink great draughts.
s.f. Grape; a raisin
adj. & s.m. Good; fit, able, qualified; — company, throng, army, row (of men); — essence (of anything).
more: 1. adj. Good. 2. s. An army or row (of men), the essence (of any thing). ¶ duḵẖ, s. 1. (for دخت) Daughter. 2. An aquatick plant (rush?) of which mats are made, a reed of which mats are formed.

def: n. f. S. द्रात्रा A raisin ( kishmish ); a grape. Chane chiraunjī ho gaë, gehūṅ ho gaë dākh, Ghar meṅ gahne tīn haiṅ, charkhā, pīṛī, khāṭ . Dohā. Gram's now as dear as nuts, wheat's grown as dear as raisins full, Three jewels but to sell are left—my spindle, bed, and stool. dākh-ras , n. m. Grape-juice; wine. dākh-latā , n. f. A vinery ( angūr kī bel ).

ety: act. part. n. of دخل 'to enter,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Entering, penetrating; arriving; — entered; inserted; included, comprehended; produced; forthcoming; — interior, inside; — entering (upon), taking possession (of); — entry (in a book or account); inclusion (of a minor in a larger piece of land, &c.)
more: adj. دخل entered. 1. Entering; penetrating; arriving. 2. Included or comprehended in. 3. Inserted; entered; registered. 4. Filed. dā/?/khil , n. m. 1. Inclusion of a minor in a larger piece of land. 2. An entry in a book or account. 3. Taking possession (of land).

(from دخل) part. act. 1. Entering, penetrating, arriving. 2. (adjectively) Entered, produced. 3. (substantively) Inside, interiour part; within. داخل خارج کرنا dāḵẖil ḵẖārij karnā, To transfer property (by taking out the name of the former proprietor from a deed or register and inserting that of the new). داخل کرنا dāḵẖil karnā, To cause to enter or arrive, to place or fix in; to produce, to include, to insert or introduce. داخل هونا dāḵẖil honā, To enter, to arrive, to penetrate, to belong, to appertain, to be inserted.
subs: s.m. 'Inserting and striking out'; a transfer of land or property under one name to another name in a deed or register; erasure of an entry; — dāḵẖil-ḵẖārij karnā, To transfer land or property (by taking out the name of the former proprietor from a deed or register and inserting that of the new)
adj. Entered upon the record, placed on record; filed with the record; laid aside, or indefinitely postponed; struck off the file
v.t. To file with the record, &c.

(-meṅ, or -ke andar), To cause to enter or be entered; to place or fix (in); to cause to arrive; to produce; to incorporate, include; to admit, lodge; to enter, insert, introduce; to enrol, enlist; to file; to deposit, to carry to credit; to pay (in); — to effect penetration
dāḵẖil-kunanda, s.m. One who enters or inserts, &c.; one who pays (in), payer, depositor
s.m. A warrant or deed of possession; a conveyance

(-meṅ), To enter, to come or go (in); to arrive; to penetrate; to enter (upon), take possession (of), to occupy; to come (under), to belong or appertain (to); — to be entered, be inserted, be enrolled, &c.; to be filed; to be paid (in), to be deposited.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَاخِل (dāxil), borrowed from Arabic دَاخِل (dāḵil).

def: adj. & s.m. Allowed entrance, admitted; introduced, adopted (as a word); occupying, in possession (of land, &c.); — intimate, familiar; — meddling, interfering; — one who has entered on possession (of); — one who has a firm footing (in any business, post, or office, &c.); — an adventive abider (among a people); a guest; an intimate, a confidant; an accomplice; — one who interferes or intermeddles, a meddler
more: adj. Occupying; possessing. dakhīl-kār , 1. An occupant; a hereditary occupant. 2. One who has authority to interfere. dakhīl-kārī , 1. Possession. 2. Hereditary occupancy.
(from دخل) 1. adj. Intimate, familiar. 2. Admitted, allowed entrance; adopted (a word). 3. s. m. A friend, a confidant, an accomplice. دخيل هونا daḵẖīl honā, To meddle, to intermeddle (in).
subs: adj. & s.m. In possession (of lands, &c.); — an occupant; — one who has authority to interfere
s.f. The being in possession (of), possession, occupation, occupancy
v.t. To allow (one) to enter (in, -meṅ), to admit; to put (one) in possession (of)
(-meṅ), To enter (upon), to occupy; to be introduced or adopted (as a word); to be a meddler, to meddle, interfere (in).

ety: for A. داخلة dāḵẖilat, fem. of dāḵẖil
def: adj. & s.m. Interior, inside; — entry (in an account, &c.); insertion; quotation; entrance; entrance fee; admittance, admission; delivery or payment (of money, &c.); a receipt (for money, — esp. for revenue paid in)
more: (properly داخله from دخل) s. m. A receipt for money, &c.

ety: for A. داخلة dāḵẖilat, fem. of dāḵẖil
def: adj. & s.m. Interior, inside; — entry (in an account, &c.); insertion; quotation; entrance; entrance fee; admittance, admission; delivery or payment (of money, &c.); a receipt (for money, — esp. for revenue paid in)
more: n. m. See dā/?/khil , (2.). 1. Delivery or payment of money, etc. 2. Admittance; admission; insertion. 3. Entrance; entrance fee. 4. A receipt, esp. a receipt for revenue payments. Hālā dīyā, dākhlā pāyā . Rus. zāviya-i-dākhilā ; H. bhītrī konā , n. m. ( Math .) An interior angle.
s. m. Entry in an account, insertion, quotation.

def: adj. Interior, internal; home (as opp. to foreign); — included, contained (in); inclusive; belonging (to); inherent
more: adj. Inclusive; contained; belonging to. dākhilī mauzā , A newly formed village attached to or included in the older ( aslī ) village. umūr-i-dākhlī o khārajī , Domestic and foreign affairs.
(from داخل) adj. Contained, belonging to, inherent.
subs: s.m. A newly formed village attached to or included in an older (aṣlī) village.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَاخِلِی (dāxilī), from Arabic دَاخِلِيّ (dāḵiliyy). First attested in 1799 as داخلی (daxly /⁠dāxilī⁠/).

def: adj. & s.m. Southern, south; — the south; the southern part of Hindūstān, the Deccan.
more: n. m. S. दक्षिण Pr. dikkhaṛo ; Pālī, dakkhino . 1. South ( junūb .) 1. Dakhin disā mati jāo, more suāmī dakhin kī nārī siyānī, Ratiyāṅ sutāveṅ, saīyāṅ, lāli palaṅg par, dinvāṅ bharāvat pānī . Bhoj. song. My husband, go not South! the women are so crafty there, Bright bed for rest by night, by day they 'll make you water bear. 2. Mūṅg phalī! dakkhan kā mevā! Delhi Street cry. Ground nuts! from the South! 2. The southern part of India.
s. m. South; the southern part of India.

def: adj. Southern; of or belonging to southern India or the Deccan (generally applied to persons or things from the south); — dakhinī podīna, s.m. Horse-mint
more: adj. S. दक्षिणीय 1. Southern ( dakshinī ). 2. Inhabiting the Deccan. dakhnī podīnah , n. Horsemint. (?) dakhnī ṭhairāo , Winter Solstice. dakhnī dhurā ; A. qutb-i-janūbī , The south pole. dakhnī rās . Southern signs, or constellation. dakhnī yā safaid mirch , White pepper. dakhnāyan-sūraj , n. The sun's southing.
adj. Southern (generally, things or persons from the south). دکهني پودينه dakhanī pūdīna, Horsemint.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of دخل 'to come in'
def: s.m. Entrance, ingress, admission, access; entering (upon), taking possession (of), possession, occupation, occupancy; making way or progress (in a study), progress, proficiency, knowledge; reach, grasp, scope, comprehension; possibility; capacity, competency; influence, power, authority, jurisdiction; — intrusion, interference, meddling, disturbance, molestation; — income, receipt, revenue, profit, produce, proceeds
more: n. m. 1. See āmdanī 1, ikhtiyār , 6, 7, and pahuṅch , 2, 3, 4. 2. Occupancy; occupation; possession. 3. Skill; proficiency; knowledge. Tum ko kuchh Aṅgrezī meṅ bhī dakhl hai . 4. Interference; meddling. 5. Disturbance; molestation. 6. The influence of an evil spirit. Bhūt kā dakhl hai. dakhl bil-jabr , n. m. Forcible or violent entry or possession. dakhl-i-bejā, dakhl-i-nā-jāiz , Unlawful entry; trespass. dakhl be-sabab, yā be-ijāzat , n. m. Entering a place without leave; intrusion. dakhl pānā , v. n. 1. To have access; have the entree ; to be admitted. 2. To obtain or be put in possession. dakhl dar māqūlāt ; H. bīch meṅ bolnā , Interruption; interference; intermeddling. dakhl-dihānī , Ped. n. f. Delivery of possession.

s. m. 1. Entrance, access; possibility. 2. Intrusion, interference, disturbance, molestation. 3. Produce, income. دخل پانا daḵẖl pānā, v. a. To have access. دخل کرنا daḵẖl karnā, v. a. To meddle, to intermeddle, to interfere, concern oneʼs self, intrude, to interrupt, to take possession, to enter, encroach.
subs: v.n. To have access, to be admitted; to obtain possession, be put in possession (of)
s.m. One who has admission (to); a partner, an accomplice
(-ko), To give admission (to), to allow to enter; to interpose (in or between, -meṅ), to interfere; to interrupt, to disturb
(-par), To enter (upon), take possession (of), to occupy; to encroach (upon); — to meddle (in, -meṅ), intermeddle, interfere (in or with), concern (oneself), intrude; to interrupt
s.m. An occupier, occupant; an encroacher, an intruder, &c.
v.t. To keep possession of, to retain
s.m. A document giving the right of occupancy, a writ or warrant of entrance or of possession (of lands, &c.)
s.f. Obtaining entrance or admission; taking possession
v.t. To exclude; to dispossess, dislodge, eject

def: n. m. A document giving the right of occupancy; a writ or warrant of entrance or possession (of lands, etc.). dakhl o qabzā, qabz o dakhl , Entry and possession.
